title: Crispy Golden Polish Banana Curry
description: Earthy, light, sugar‑free Polish dish with pan‑fried ripe bananas slow‑cooked in aromatic spices and coconut milk.
instructions: Heat the butter in a large skillet over medium heat until melted and foamy.
Add the sliced bananas and fry for 2–3 minutes per side until each slice is golden brown; transfer to a plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, stir in the ginger, cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, and salt; cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the coconut milk, whisking to combine, and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add the coconut flour and whisk until the sauce thickens slightly, about 2 minutes.

Return the fried bananas to the skillet, gently folding them into the sauce to coat.
Simmer the curry on low heat for an additional 15–20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken.
Serve the curry warm, optionally garnished with fresh coriander or a squeeze of lime for brightness (not included in ingredient list).
